raw_input:
[5.0/5] Works great! It works really well, even in the dry and frigid winter air. Non-greasy and moisture rich. [4.0/5] Korean serum that delivers gentle hydration with ceramides and squalane This Skin & Lab Milky Serum is lightweight and unscented. I like the texture and feel of this serum - not sticky nor greasy but offers immediate hydration. It leaves the skin feeling soft and well hydrated. The serum contains squalane and ceramides, providing moisture below the surface. [5.0/5] Very Hydrating This serum is a milky looking, thin serum that goes on easily and absorbs quickly. After applying, my skin looks hydrated but a bit shiny, probably a result of the squalane.

The serum is ultra hydrating and after 5 days of use, a few dry patches of skin, that nothing had helped, are no longer visible, even after washing my face. [5.0/5] Soothing & Skin Feels Less Sensitive, Hydrates & Improves Skin Texture, Free Of Fragrance/Alcohol Skin & Lab Barrierderm Milky Serum Milk Ceramide Complex is a milky serum free of fragrance and alcohol that comes in an easy to use dropper bottle. Ceramides can make skin healthier by strengthening the skin barrier, preventing moisture loss and protecting against environmental damage. Also, ceramides work better combined with certain types of ingredients that are contained in this serum, such as cholesterol, glycerin, squalane (rich in fatty acids) and more.

This serum feels wonderful on my dry, sensitive and mature (55+) skin that needs a lot of hydration and is aggravated easily by chemicals fragrance, natural fragrancing essential oils and extracts, drying alcohol, aggressive products, the environment and so on. I’ve been using this ceramide serum after cleansing and follow with my face cream, but it also layers well with my favorite staples, such as a Vitamin C and hyaluronic acid serum (followed by my moisturizer).

I like that this ceramide serum doesn’t appear to have any harsh ingredients, and it feels soothing and gentle to my skin. I’ve used it about a week so far and it adds noticeable hydration that isn't heavy, greasy or sticky, and my normally sensitive skin is calm and comfortable. Also, my skin texture feels soft and seems more elastic. I can’t judge the full benefits yet as that can take many weeks or months, but I’m pleased so far and will keep using this serum. Love it. 5 stars. [5.0/5] Nice gentle serum Very nice gentle, hydrating serum. I have dry sensitive skin and this serum is great! Very light scent and makes my skin very soft. [5.0/5] Wonderful Product! It's called 'milky serum' so I don't know why I was so surprised at the fact the texture is well... milky!

It's lightweight, but not so light as say, a toner. It came with a dropper applicator which really grew on me over a few days, and while it's tacky when you first put it on, it really absorbs extremely well after a minute or two.

This has become a steady part of my morning routine, and it definitely makes a difference. My skin looks and feels great and I'm definitely going to continue using it. [4.0/5] Light Serum I love that it’s fragrance free and cruelty free. This serum is milky white in color. To me, it’s more of a moisturizer lotion than a serum. It spreads over the skin very smoothly, almost slippery, so you don’t need a lot to cover your face. It’s light weight yet very hydrating. I didn’t have any bad reaction from it. Another good Korean skincare product. I recommend it. [5.0/5] I feel rejuvenated! I am now 44 and have to admit that my skin will never be like it was in my 20's! I have been using the same moisturizer routine for a very long time with the same products and I have not been seeing any difference over the years.

This milky serum has turned the clock back for me and I am very happy with the results!

I replaced my oil free night time moisturizer with the Milky Serum and in only a few days I am seeing my dark spots fading and my fine lines are disappearing! I am outside all summer and even with SPF I still suffer from skin damage on my temples. I am shocked that this lotion has made those spots disappear!
I apply a dropper full to my forehead, cheeks and neck and gently stroke upward and the serum absorbs quickly with no stickiness. It has virtually no odor which is great because I am sensitive to perfumes.

This is a great product for the price and if you are looking to renew your aging skin I highly recommend! [4.0/5] Nice, light moisturizer When I first tried this serum, I wasn't sure I liked it...but it has grown on me. The serum is well-described as being milky. It's thin and white...so much so that I found it hard to believe it could be much of a moisturizer. And when I put it on my face, I noticed it had a bit of a tack/stickiness to it. It wasn't overwhelming like glue, but if I put my hands to my face, I noticed that tacky feeling. Again, these were not things I was used to in a moisturizer.

The moisturizer is fairly quickly absorbed; however, and that tackiness goes away within a minute or so. What you're left with is a nice smooth feeling. I don’t know that my face feels overly moisturized, but it feels healthy. I also tested it on one of my elbows (which has just a bit of eczema), and I believe the eczema has improved beyond what it was looking like with shea butter applications alone.

My wife has been using this serum as well, and she would give it 3 or 4 stars...while my opinion is 4 or 5. Four stars then seems fair. I love that this serum has its origins in Korea, as I trust their beauty standards. And admittedly I would say my feeling that the serum was a bit unusual was tempered by the product's origins. Again, I trust their beauty standards and somewhat assume I'm filling a hole I didn't know was there. A couple weeks in, I'm not yet completely convinced that I will make this or a similar product a standard in my skin care regimen. I nevertheless love the idea of rotating products/varying what you apply to your skin (to again cover any holes the products you've been using may be missing), so I'm very glad I tried it. If I notice any outstanding results (one way or another), I will provide an update. [5.0/5] No breakout with this amazing serum! First time trying out this serum and love it! It absorbs into your skin quickly and no breakout for my sensitive skin. Will definitely order again! [5.0/5] Nice quality moisturizer I've tried a few different moisturizers in the last couple of months and this one is my favorite so far! I just use the dropper and add a little to my fingertips then dot it all over my face. It seems to absorb quickly and leaves no tackiness or smell behind. Leaves my skin feeling smooth and hydrated! I do have trouble with flare ups of seborrheic dermatitis on my face and this doesn't seem to aggravate it at all. I'd say it's probably worth a try for most people with sensitive skin. [5.0/5] Nice light milky serum from Korea! If you are looking for a nice, light high-quality serum moisturizer, this likely fits the bill. I have recently learned that Korea has very high standards for beauty and skin products with strict government regulations as to safety and quality.

This product meets expectations. It is light and absorbs easily. It is not at all greasy -- very light. There is a very slight fragrance that is not noticeable at all on my face. I found it unusual that a serum would be a milky white rather than clear, but it absorbs like typical serums. I put the droplets into my palm, then dot on my face and smooth it in, especially in the areas where I have lines and wrinkles. I let it absorb and dry for a few minutes, and then apply my moisturizer over it. I have had no sensitivity issues with this product.

I am very pleased with this product so far! [5.0/5] Milky Moisturizing Serum This milky serum really delivers on providing moisturization and healthy glow to my skin, especially in this cold weather season. I have oily/combo skin, however, during the cold season, I have oily yet tight parched skin and need some time of serum or moisturizer that helps relieve the tightness without leaving any oily residue, and this does the job. [4.0/5] Has a really great smell Pretty thin, so I reckon this will last a good deal of time, the lotion does a pretty good job of moisturizing the skin.
Love the smell...its the best part, though admittedly its a good moisturizer as well and it didn't leave a greasy film
We liked that it had a nice dropper dispenser too, so you don't have to worry about accidentally pulling too much out of the bottle. [4.0/5] Hello, moisturizing serum, and good-bye, dry skin! I was already prone to dry skin before relocating to a desert climate where I was forced to really up my hydration game. I have gone through numerous products that claim to help but then end up w/ minimal (if any) improvement.

After day 1, I wasn't sold on this product either. However, I decided to continue its use for at least five days since the product description claimed noticeable improvement "after 5 days." I am glad that I stuck it out.

I initially thought the eye dropper weird but quickly grew to appreciate it. A small dose goes a long way and the eye dropper allows easier control to obtain only the bare minimum product necessary without wasting anything. I do wish that it was slightly larger as the bulb does not fill completely, so I require a refill (or two) to cover the entirety of my face, but this is a minor nuisance at most.

This product is unscented (a plus!) and smooths easily across the face. It is somewhat noticeable for a short timeframe after application. It is semi-shiny and not sticky but maybe slightly tacky (?not sure how best to describe this). If you follow the product description's instructions to "gently pat for better absorption," there is less of this feeling but it is still noticeably present. I do not wear make-up, but I imagine that those who do might need to ensure sufficient time has passed after use before dolling themselves up (if applying in the morning).

There seemed to be some mild improvement after a couple days, but there was no question about its effectiveness after the aforementioned five days. My skin was definitely smoother and improved! I look forward to continuing its use in my facial care regimen and hope to try out some of this other brand's products. [5.0/5] Light, moisturizing, unscented This is a very lightweight (almost watery) serum. I do not detect a scent, which is great since I find many fragrances overpowering in moisturizers. It absorbs quickly and feels nice when applied. The applicator is a bit odd—it’s a syringe type applicator with a bulb end. I assume it is dispensed this way because the consistency is so watery. I just apply the wand directly to my face and then massage the product in with my fingers. So far, I’m pleased with the moisturizing results. I assume any anti-aging benefit will take a while to discern. [5.0/5] A must have There is no scent to this serum. The consistency is like a watery cream. It's very thin and once applied to my face, it's light and doesn't have any scent. This is my last step to my skin routine and it does wonders. It claims it acts like a barrier and I totally agree. Once it has absorbed into my face, it feels like a silky veil, leaving my skin soft and glowy. I'm very pleased with this serum. [4.0/5] Gentle, Soothing Moisture Being that my facial skin changes greatly during the winter months, I thought this serum might help to re-establish some moisture while battling my winter dryness. This review is for: [SKIN&LAB] Barrierderm Milky Serum | Infused with Milk Ceramide Complex, Squalane, and Panthenol to ...

I have used products from Skin & Lab before and liked most of them, and this serum is no exception. I was coming off of a different moisturizer that really irritated my skin, so I introduced this serum slowly. I started using it only once a day and once that previous irritation cleared up I moved up to twice a day.

This serum is somewhere in between watery and a cream. The glass bottle is opaque and the serum does dispense as milky-looking. It has no scent that I can detect. The dropper dispenses enough for about half my face, so I've been using two droppers per application. It has soothed my skin, clearing up my previous Rosacea break out and smoothing out my skin. I have an oily t-zone and a super dry chin this winter, and all of that seems to be gone now.

Due to these cold, breezy winter months, I have been using a moisturizer over this serum. I suspect that in the summer months, I might not even need to do that.

I'm looking forward to continued use of this product and when it's gone I will most likely buy more. The price is affordable and this works wonderfully on my dry, winter skin. [5.0/5] Wonderful Serum This review is for [SKIN&LAB] Barrierderm Milky Serum.

It's great for me. My dry sensitive skin stays moist throughout the day and looks healthy and well-nourished, and it never feels irritated. This serum is lightweight and comfortable to wear and so I use it night and day.

response:
The Skin & Lab Barrierderm Milky Serum receives overwhelmingly positive reviews, with most customers giving it 4 or 5 stars. Reviewers consistently praise its lightweight, milky texture that absorbs quickly without leaving a greasy or sticky residue. The serum is widely appreciated for being fragrance-free and free of harsh ingredients, making it a popular choice for people with dry, sensitive, or mature skin.

Customers highlight its strong hydrating properties, with several noting visible improvements in dry patches, skin texture, and fine lines after just a few days of use. The ceramide and squalane formula is frequently mentioned as a key reason for its effectiveness in strengthening the skin barrier and locking in moisture. Some users also report improvements in conditions like rosacea and eczema.

The dropper applicator receives mixed but mostly positive feedback. Some found it unusual at first but grew to appreciate the control it offers, while a few noted the bulb does not always fill completely, requiring multiple draws to cover the whole face.

A small number of reviewers noted a slight tackiness immediately after application, though this typically fades within a minute or two. One reviewer mentioned a light scent, while most others detected none at all.

Overall, customers across various skin types, including oily, combination, dry, and sensitive, find this serum to be a gentle, effective, and affordable addition to their skincare routines, with many stating they plan to repurchase.
